title: Anyone interested in traslating a few books
display_title: Anyone interested in traslating a few books
wikitext:
[]: Anyone interested in traslating a few books.             Quote Post by berto » Fri Jun 04, 2010 7:34 pm I have two books, two very rare books, I don't think you'll find them anywhere, I was lucky to get them at all. One, according to the discription the seller made on the page, is a collection of essays by a feminist regarding the themes and images in the movie and show. The book is called A New Millenium Perspective on the Daughters of Eve , most of it is in Japanese but it does have a lot of English sections including one essay called Evangelion as the Immaculate Virgin . The other is a book is completly in Japanese and is a study on the 'puzzels' of the show. I've had these books for a while and I've keep them in a safe spot and found them the other day and figuered that I could provably share them and, maybe, someone'd be interested in translating them. So I talked to the guy at evaotaku.com and asked him if he was interested in this but he told me that he wasn't actively doing anything eva related and told me that if anyone was interested this would be the place to go. I don't know is anyone is interested in translating the books but I'd be willing to scan them, or provably photograph 'em.

[]: Re: Anyone interested in traslating a few books.             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jun 04, 2010 8:27 pm We're hard up for people to translate big things such as ANIMA and various Anno interviews, so I wouldn't count on seeing these translated for a while. berto wrote: Evangelion as the Immaculate Virgin

[]: Re: Anyone interested in traslating a few books.             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Jun 04, 2010 10:41 pm berto wrote: I have two books, two very rare books, I don't think you'll find them anywhere, I was lucky to get them at all.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Sat Jun 05, 2010 2:45 am I think I might've fraced that poorly. I didn't mean you'd never find them even if you looked for them, I mean you'd never run into them just by browsing around. These were never popular publishings so there would be a very high provability of never finding them unless you were activly looking for them. I don't have any way of scanning the covers, it would take me some time to get to a scaner which why I asked first, so that I'd know I wasn't waisting my time posting them if no one was interested in even looking at them. The rest is easy: 新世紀エヴァンゲリオンの謎 ISBN4-8454-0529-6 Published: 1997 and 聖母エヴァンゲリオン―A new millennialist perspective on the daughters of Eve ISBN4-8387-0917-X Published: 1997 (More information here:[url]http://www.evamonkey.com/writings_horn13.php[/url]) I should warn you, these books are around 200 pages each, with the ocational pretty picture, thats 400 pages of Japanese text. This is not a request to translate, per say, I'm asking if anyone is interested in translating because I thought people would find the material interesting, but I wouldn't go as far as actively requesting a translation. I've done translation work from spanish to english and vice versa, so I know that language to language translations are not easy; it's an art not a science. So blatenly asking someone to just translate something like this seems rather blaten to me. That's why I'm asking if there is any interest first rather than an actual request to translate.
